methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Anthrogen is a Seed-stage biotech company founded by Ankit Singhal that has raised $4.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including public peers RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L, and notable private comparables such as Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

A single applicable valuation method supports this analysis: Stage-Progression. Using the $4.5M total raised as a baseline value proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $11M. Peer-Median triangulation is inapplicable due to insufficient narrow peer density—while 16 same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage comps with similar valuation tiers yielded fewer than the three datapoints required for statistical reliability. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied because Anthrogen's revenue is not disclosed, precluding use of the 7.4x median P/S multiple observed across the three public biotech comparables.

The triangulated valuation range is $10M to $13M, with a median point estimate of $11M. This analysis carries meaningful uncertainty: the single methodology reduces confidence, and the absence of revenue disclosure prevents cross-check via public multiples. Key unknowns include Anthrogen's revenue trajectory, pipeline stage, and competitive positioning relative to the identified private peer set.
